title: Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu

seo.title: Spicy Korean Braised Tofu (Dubu Jorim) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make delicious Spicy Korean Braised Tofu (Dubu Jorim) at home! This easy, authentic vegetarian recipe is packed with flavor. Get cooking now!
longTailKeywords: how to make spicy Korean braised tofu
easy spicy Korean braised tofu recipe
seo.h1: Spicy Korean Braised Tofu (Dubu Jorim) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Spicy Korean Braised Tofu (Dubu Jorim)?
How to Make Spicy Braised Tofu: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Dubu Jorim
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of spicy Korean braised tofu (Dubu Jorim) garnished with green onions and sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this flavorful and easy-to-make spicy Korean braised tofu, a perfect vegetarian meal.

description: Dubu Jorim is a beloved Korean dish featuring pan-fried tofu simmered in a vibrant, spicy, and savory sauce. This vegetarian delight offers a perfect balance of heat and umami, making it a comforting and satisfying meal. It is an excellent way to enjoy tofu, absorbing all the rich flavors of the braising liquid.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tofu
instructions.text: Drain the tofu and press out excess water using paper towels or a tofu press for at least 15-20 minutes. Slice the pressed tofu into 1/2-inch thick rectangles. This step helps the tofu absorb flavors better and gives it a firmer texture.

instructions.title: Make the Braising Sauce
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the soy sauce, gochugaru, minced garlic, sesame oil, and sugar. Whisk well until the sugar is dissolved and all ingredients are thoroughly mixed. Set aside.

instructions.title: Pan-Fry the Tofu
instructions.text: Heat the neutral cooking oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Carefully place the tofu slices in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Fry for 3-4 minutes per side, or until golden brown and slightly crispy. Remove the tofu from the pan and set aside on a plate lined with paper towels to drain any excess oil.

instructions.title: Combine Tofu and Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully return the fried tofu slices to the same skillet in a single layer. Pour the prepared braising sauce evenly over the tofu. Add the water or vegetable broth to the skillet.

instructions.title: Simmer and Braise
instructions.text: Bring the sauce to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low. Let the tofu simmer, uncovered, for about 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and reduced, coating the tofu nicely. Gently flip the tofu slices halfway through simmering to ensure even coating.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the sauce has reached your desired consistency, remove the skillet from the heat. Garnish generously with sliced green onions and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds. Serve immediately with a bowl of warm rice and other Korean side dishes.

tips: Pressing the tofu thoroughly is crucial for a firm texture and better flavor absorption. Dont skip this step!
Adjust the amount of gochugaru to suit your spice preference. For a milder dish, reduce it to 1 tablespoon; for extra heat, add up to 2.5 tablespoons.
For a richer flavor, you can substitute a portion of the water with kelp broth (dashima broth) or mushroom broth.
article:
Dubu Jorim, also known as Spicy Braised Tofu, is a cornerstone of Korean cuisine that beautifully showcases how simple ingredients can be transformed into a dish bursting with flavor and comfort. For vegetarians and vegans, it’s a revelation – a protein-packed, satisfying, and incredibly versatile dish that can serve as a hearty main or a delightful banchan (side dish). If you're looking to explore the vibrant and healthful world of Korean food, this spicy, savory, and slightly sweet tofu dish is an excellent place to start. It’s not just food; it’s an experience that tantalizes the taste buds and nourishes the soul, proving that plant-based meals can be utterly delicious and deeply satisfying.

## What is Spicy Korean Braised Tofu (Dubu Jorim)?

Dubu Jorim literally translates to "tofu braised in sauce." "Dubu" means tofu, and "Jorim" refers to a cooking technique where ingredients are simmered in a seasoned sauce until the liquid reduces and the flavors concentrate. This particular dish features firm or extra-firm tofu pan-fried until golden, then gently simmered in a robust, spicy, and savory sauce primarily made from gochugaru (Korean chili flakes), soy sauce, garlic, and often a touch of sweetness. The result is tender tofu that has absorbed all the complex flavors of the sauce, offering a delightful texture and an irresistible taste.

Spicy Korean Braised Tofu is celebrated for its perfect balance of heat, umami, and a subtle sweetness that cuts through the spice. It’s a dish that exemplifies the Korean philosophy of harmonious flavors, making it a beloved banchan served alongside rice and other dishes, or even a standalone main course for a lighter meal. Its vegetarian nature makes it incredibly popular, offering a healthy and protein-rich alternative that doesn't compromise on flavor.

## Ingredients for Dubu Jorim

The beauty of Dubu Jorim lies in its relatively simple ingredient list, most of which are staples in Korean pantries. Here’s what you’ll need to create this fantastic Spicy Korean Braised Tofu:

*   **Firm or Extra-Firm Tofu:** This is crucial. Softer tofu varieties will crumble during braising. Look for blocks weighing around 14-16 ounces (400-450g).
*   **Aromatics:**
    *   **Garlic:** Plenty of minced garlic provides a pungent, essential base flavor.
    *   **Green Onions (Scallions):** Used in the sauce and for garnish, adding a fresh, oniony bite.
    *   **Optional:** A small amount of minced onion or ginger can add depth.
*   **The Signature Spicy Sauce:**
    *   **Gochugaru (Korean Chili Flakes):** The star of the show. This gives Dubu Jorim its characteristic vibrant red color and its unique, smoky heat. Adjust the amount to your spice preference.
    *   **Soy Sauce:** Provides the fundamental savory, umami base.
    *   **Sesame Oil:** Adds a rich, nutty aroma and flavor.
    *   **Sugar or Rice Syrup:** Balances the spice and saltiness with a touch of sweetness.
    *   **Water or Vegetable Broth:** The liquid for braising, allowing the tofu to absorb the flavors.
    *   **Optional:** Gochujang (Korean chili paste) for a deeper, fermented chili flavor and a thicker sauce, or mirin (rice wine) for sweetness and to round out flavors.
*   **Cooking Oil:** For pan-frying the tofu.
*   **Garnish:** Toasted sesame seeds and extra chopped green onions for a final flourish.

## How to Make Spicy Braised Tofu: Step-by-Step

Making Dubu Jorim is a straightforward process that yields incredibly rewarding results. Follow these steps for a delicious batch of Spicy Korean Braised Tofu:

1.  **Prepare the Tofu:**
    *   **Press the Tofu:** This is a vital step. Wrap the block of tofu in paper towels or a clean kitchen towel. Place it on a plate and put something heavy on top (like a cutting board with cans) for at least 30 minutes, or up to an hour. This removes excess water, allowing the tofu to absorb the sauce better and giving it a firmer texture.
    *   **Slice and Pan-Fry:** Once pressed, slice the tofu into rectangular pieces, about 1/2 inch thick. Heat a thin layer of cooking oil in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Pan-fry the tofu slices for 3-5 minutes per side until golden brown and slightly crispy. This step adds texture and helps the tofu hold its shape during braising. Set aside.

2.  **Make the Braising Sauce:**
    *   In a medium bowl, combine all the sauce ingredients: gochugaru, soy sauce, minced garlic, sesame oil, sugar/rice syrup, and water/vegetable broth. Whisk well until everything is thoroughly mixed. Taste and adjust seasonings – add more gochugaru for extra spice, or more sugar for sweetness.

3.  **Braise the Tofu:**
    *   Arrange the pan-fried tofu slices in a single layer in a shallow pan or skillet. Pour the prepared sauce evenly over the tofu.
    *   Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer over medium heat. Once simmering, reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and let it braise for 10-15 minutes. This allows the tofu to fully absorb the flavors of the sauce.

4.  **Reduce and Garnish:**
    *   After braising, uncover the pan and increase the heat slightly to medium-low. Continue to simmer, gently spooning the sauce over the tofu occasionally, until the sauce thickens and reduces to your desired consistency. This usually takes another 5-10 minutes. Be careful not to let the sauce dry out completely.
    *   Remove from heat. Garnish generously with chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds before serving.

## Tips for the Best Dubu Jorim

To truly master Spicy Korean Braised Tofu and achieve that perfect balance of flavor and texture, keep these tips in mind:

*   **Don't Skip Pressing the Tofu:** This is the most crucial step for texture. It prevents watery tofu and ensures maximum flavor absorption.
*   **Pan-Frying is Key:** While optional, pan-frying the tofu before braising creates a delightful golden crust, adds a chewy texture, and helps the tofu maintain its shape, preventing it from crumbling during simmering.
*   **Adjust Spice to Your Liking:** The amount of gochugaru can be easily adjusted. Start with a moderate amount and add more if you prefer a spicier kick. For less heat, you can reduce the gochugaru and perhaps add a pinch of paprika for color.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Always taste your sauce before and during braising. You might want to add a bit more soy sauce for saltiness, sugar for sweetness, or gochugaru for heat.
*   **Let it Rest (Optional but Recommended):** Like many braised dishes, Dubu Jorim often tastes even better after sitting for a bit, allowing the flavors to meld and deepen. It's fantastic served warm or at room temperature.

## Serving Suggestions & Variations

Spicy Korean Braised Tofu is incredibly versatile. It's most commonly served as a banchan (side dish) alongside a bowl of steaming rice and other Korean dishes. Its rich flavor and satisfying texture make it a perfect accompaniment to lighter soups or grilled meats.

For a complete vegetarian meal, you can serve Dubu Jorim as the main course with rice and a simple fresh salad. It's also excellent for meal prepping, as it keeps well in the refrigerator and its flavors often improve overnight.

**Variations to explore:**

*   **Add Vegetables:** Enhance the dish by braising other quick-cooking vegetables alongside the tofu. Sliced onions, zucchini, mushrooms, bell peppers, or even a handful of spinach can be added during the last few minutes of simmering.
*   **Sweeter or Less Spicy:** Adjust the sugar and gochugaru ratios to suit your palate. For a milder version, use less gochugaru and add a little extra sugar or mirin.
*   **Thicker Sauce:** If you prefer a thicker, clingier sauce, you can add a small amount of cornstarch slurry (cornstarch mixed with a little water) during the last few minutes of simmering.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is Dubu Jorim vegan?**
Yes, traditional Dubu Jorim made with the ingredients listed (soy sauce, gochugaru, etc.) is naturally vegan. Ensure your soy sauce does not contain any animal products (most don't, but it's good to check).

**Can I make Dubu Jorim less spicy?**
Absolutely! The spice level is primarily controlled by the amount of gochugaru. Start with a smaller amount (e.g., 1 tablespoon) and taste the sauce. You can always add more if you prefer a spicier dish.

**How long does Dubu Jorim last in the refrigerator?**
When stored in an airtight container, Spicy Korean Braised Tofu will keep well in the refrigerator for 3-4 days. It’s a great make-ahead dish!

**Can I use silken tofu for Dubu Jorim?**
It's not recommended. Silken tofu is too delicate and will likely fall apart during the pressing and braising process. Firm or extra-firm tofu is essential for maintaining structure.

**What if I don't have gochugaru?**
While gochugaru is crucial for authentic flavor and color, if you're in a pinch, you can try a blend of cayenne pepper and paprika (about 2:1 ratio) to mimic the heat and color, but the unique smoky and fruity notes of gochugaru will be missed. It’s worth seeking out genuine Korean chili flakes for the best results.

Embrace the delicious world of Spicy Korean Braised Tofu and discover why this simple yet profoundly flavorful dish has become a beloved staple in Korean households and among food enthusiasts worldwide. Its ease of preparation, health benefits, and incredible taste make it a must-try for anyone looking to add exciting new flavors to their culinary repertoire.
